name: Sumit Yadav
affiliation: Computer Engineering graduate · Pulchowk Campus, Tribhuvan University, Nepal
bioSummary: Hello! I'm an AI researcher trying to understand how language models work on the inside — why a safety-aligned model sometimes refuses perfectly harmless questions, and how the geometry of a model's internal representations relates to what it has actually learned. A lot of it is simply curiosity, following a question until it starts to make sense. I care about making these tools useful for languages that are often left out: my family speaks Maithili, so building a small language model for it felt as personal as it was technical. Alongside research I work on AI safety and agentic systems at Astha.ai, and I hope to pursue a PhD in interpretability and alignment. I try to learn in the open — I write here, and share short explainers on YouTube.
